status_reason: 1 hypothesis passed Quality Gate (CONDITIONAL PASS). C2-3 pyocyanin/DHODH ferroptosis is web-verified novel with 82% groundedness, but has cell-type counter-evidence (Muller 2016).

selected_target.field_a: Ferroptosis biology -- iron-dependent regulated cell death via lipid peroxidation (4-HNE, ox-PE, GPX4, System Xc-)
selected_target.field_c: Bacterial quorum sensing biochemistry -- acyl-homoserine lactone (AHL) signaling, autoinducer synthesis, LuxR-family receptors
bridge_concepts: 4-HNE structural similarity to short-chain AHLs (alpha,beta-unsaturated carbonyls)
ox-PE threshold dynamics paralleling quorum sensing threshold switching
LasR/RhlR receptor promiscuity for non-cognate lipid signals
GPX4 as gatekeeper for host-microbiome lipid signaling
Iron availability as shared variable (ferroptosis vs siderophores)

literature_context: Ferroptosis x QS fields are DISJOINT. No published work connects ferroptosis lipid peroxidation products to bacterial QS receptor activation. Closest work: (1) AHL effects on mammalian cells (one-directional), (2) iron competition in infection (nutritional immunity), (3) QS-immune crosstalk. Key gaps: 4-HNE as QS mimic, GPX4 as inter-kingdom gatekeeper, ferroptotic iron release as virulence trigger, threshold dynamics homology. Recent breakthroughs: FSP1/DHODH parallel ferroptosis pathways, LuxR solo receptors responding to exogenous signals, 3-oxo-C12-HSL activating host T2R38. 4-HNE has alpha,beta-unsaturated carbonyl structure similar to short-chain AHLs. LuxR receptors show broader ligand promiscuity than crystal structures suggest. Literature context compiled from parametric knowledge (literature scout agent failed); all claims need web-search verification by Critic.

raw.title: 4-HNE as a Cross-Kingdom Quorum Sensing Mimic that Activates LuxR Solo Receptors in Gut Commensals
raw.connection: Ferroptosis lipid peroxidation (4-HNE) -> Structural mimicry of short-chain AHLs -> LuxR solo receptor activation

raw.title: Ferroptotic Iron Release Creates Localized Siderophore-Independent Iron Bonanza Triggering QS Threshold Collapse in P. aeruginosa
raw.connection: Ferroptosis labile iron release -> Iron bonanza bypassing siderophore requirement -> Accelerated QS threshold activation

raw.title: GPX4 as Inter-Kingdom Signal Gatekeeper Preventing Ferroptotic 4-HNE from Activating Bacterial QS
raw.connection: GPX4 enzymatic activity -> Reduction of lipid hydroperoxides -> Prevention of QS-mimetic signal release

raw.title: ox-PE Bistable Switch Dynamics Mathematically Isomorphic to AHL QS Enabling Cross-Activation
raw.connection: Ferroptosis ox-PE threshold -> Shared bistable switch topology -> Coupled activation with bacterial QS

raw.title: Bacterial AHL Lactonases Degrade 4-HNE Cyclic Derivatives as Microbial Detoxification Service
raw.connection: Bacterial quorum quenching lactonases -> Promiscuous hydrolysis of 4-HNE cyclization products -> Host tissue protection

raw.title: Ferroptosis-Derived Isoprostanes as Host-Produced Quorum Quenching Molecules via LuxR Competitive Inhibition
raw.connection: Ferroptotic isoprostane generation -> Competitive antagonism at LuxR binding pockets -> Host quorum quenching

raw.title: ACSL4 Ferroptosis Sensitivity Under Balancing Selection from Pathogen QS-Triggered Iron Theft
raw.connection: Bacterial QS-activated virulence -> Host ferroptosis as collateral damage -> Positive selection on ACSL4

raw.title: 3-oxo-C12-HSL Directly Inhibits GPX4 via Selenocysteine Modification to Induce Host Ferroptosis
raw.connection: P. aeruginosa 3-oxo-C12-HSL -> Covalent modification of GPX4 Sec46 -> Host cell ferroptosis induction

critiqued.title: 4-HNE as a Cross-Kingdom Quorum Sensing Mimic that Activates LuxR Solo Receptors in Gut Commensals
critiqued.key_weakness: Lactone ring required for all known LuxR activation; 4-HNE half-life < 2 min; no ring-free LuxR activator known; mucus barrier blocks diffusion

critiqued.title: Ferroptotic Iron Release Creates Localized Siderophore-Independent Iron Bonanza Triggering QS Threshold Collapse in P. aeruginosa
critiqued.key_weakness: LIP may not expand during ferroptosis (2025 bioRxiv); Fur represses virulence under iron excess; field rapidly converging (2025 Nature Comms PQS paper)

critiqued.title: GPX4 as Inter-Kingdom Signal Gatekeeper Preventing Ferroptotic 4-HNE from Activating Bacterial QS
critiqued.key_weakness: Complete dependency on unproven H1; incorrect mouse model citation (Matsushita 2015 was T cells not intestinal epithelium); Occam's razor

critiqued.title: ox-PE Bistable Switch Dynamics Mathematically Isomorphic to AHL QS Enabling Cross-Activation
critiqued.key_weakness: Analogy-as-mechanism fallacy; spatial scale mismatch; POVPC structurally dissimilar to LAI-1; borders on unfalsifiability

critiqued.title: Bacterial AHL Lactonases Degrade 4-HNE Cyclic Derivatives as Microbial Detoxification Service
critiqued.key_weakness: 4-HNE cyclization produces cyclic ethers not lactones; AHL lactonases highly specific for AHLs; chemical error in mechanism

critiqued.title: Ferroptosis-Derived Isoprostanes as Host-Produced Quorum Quenching Molecules via LuxR Competitive Inhibition
critiqued.key_weakness: Isoprostanes too bulky for fully enclosed LuxR binding pocket; no non-AHL-scaffold LuxR inhibitor known; MW overlap meaningless for binding

critiqued.title: ACSL4 Ferroptosis Sensitivity Under Balancing Selection from Pathogen QS-Triggered Iron Theft
critiqued.key_weakness: Brain function selection pressure on ACSL4 dwarfs infection-driven selection; multi-link causal chain with confounders

critiqued.title: 3-oxo-C12-HSL Directly Inhibits GPX4 via Selenocysteine Modification to Induce Host Ferroptosis
critiqued.key_weakness: Direct covalent Sec46 modification unlikely (GPX4 tetrad suppresses nucleophilicity; beta-keto weak electrophile); survives via revised indirect mechanism

evolved.title: 3-oxo-C12-HSL Induces Host Ferroptosis via System Xc- Competitive Inhibition and GSH Depletion
evolved.connection: P. aeruginosa 3-oxo-C12-HSL -> SLC7A11 (System Xc-) acyl-chain competitive inhibition -> GSH depletion below GPX4 co-substrate threshold -> Lipid hydroperoxide accumulation -> Host cell ferroptosis

evolved.title: ACSL4 rs2278190 Myeloid-Specific Regulatory Variant Under Pathogen-Driven Balancing Selection in Pre-Antibiotic-Era Populations
evolved.connection: P. aeruginosa QS-activated ExoU phospholipase -> AA liberation from PE in high-ACSL4 myeloid cells -> Ferroptotic death -> Selection pressure on rs2278190

evolved.title: Gut Microbiome AHL Lactonases Hydrolyze 4-Hydroxy-Nonenoic Acid Gamma-Lactone (HNE-GL) as Novel Inter-Kingdom Detoxification
evolved.connection: Ferroptosis 4-HNE release -> ALDH3A1 oxidation to 4-HNA -> Spontaneous gamma-lactonization to HNE-GL -> AiiA/QsdA AHL lactonase hydrolysis

evolved.title: 4-HNE-Glutathione Conjugate (4-HNE-GSH) as a Stable Ring-Bearing SdiA Partial Agonist via MRP1 Export
evolved.connection: Ferroptotic GSH depletion -> 4-HNE-GSH Michael adduct -> MRP1 export -> SdiA partial agonism via gamma-glutamyl pseudo-ring

critic_questions.question: Can you propose an alternative indirect mechanism for 3-oxo-C12-HSL inducing ferroptosis (e.g., GSH depletion, iron mobilization, system Xc- inhibition) given that direct GPX4 Sec46 covalent modification is unlikely due to the catalytic tetrad suppressing selenocysteine nucleophilicity?

How does your iron bonanza model account for Fur-mediated repression of PvdS and PrrF sRNAs under iron-replete conditions, which may paradoxically SUPPRESS some QS-linked virulence genes when iron is abundant?

Given that PQS already induces macrophage ferroptosis (2025 Nature Comms), should the hypothesis set pivot to explore ferroptosis-to-QS signaling more exclusively, rather than repeating the QS-to-ferroptosis direction being actively explored?

raw.title: 3-oxo-C12-HSL Induces Ferroptosis via TRIM25-Mediated GPX4 Proteasomal Degradation, Not Direct Chemical Inhibition
raw.connection: P. aeruginosa 3-oxo-C12-HSL -> NF-kB activation -> TRIM25 E3 ligase upregulation -> GPX4 K48-linked polyubiquitination and proteasomal degradation -> Host epithelial ferroptosis
raw.critic_question_addressed: H8: alternative indirect mechanism for 3-oxo-C12-HSL inducing ferroptosis

raw.title: Ferroptotic HMGB1 Release Modulates P. aeruginosa QS via HMGB1-DNA Competitive Displacement at Lux-Box Promoters
raw.connection: Ferroptotic DAMP release (all-thiol HMGB1) -> Bacterial uptake -> HMGB1 Box-A minor groove binding at lux-box-adjacent DNA -> LasR dimer displacement -> QS transcriptional reprogramming

raw.title: Pyocyanin-Initiated Mitochondrial Lipid Peroxidation Radical Chain Reactions Induce DHODH-Pathway-Specific Ferroptosis
raw.connection: P. aeruginosa QS-regulated pyocyanin -> Mitochondrial redox cycling -> Superoxide/Fenton radical chain PUFA-PE oxidation -> DHODH-pathway overwhelmed -> Compartment-specific ferroptosis
raw.critic_question_addressed: general: novel QS-to-ferroptosis mechanism orthogonal to PQS-TFR1

raw.title: Ferroptotic 15-HpETE-PE Export via Microvesicle Shedding Activates P. aeruginosa PqsR as Non-Cognate Ligand Creating Positive Feedback Loop
raw.connection: Ferroptosis 15-LOX/PEBP1 ox-PE generation -> Microvesicle shedding -> Bacterial membrane fusion -> Phospholipase liberation of 15-HpETE -> PqsR activation -> PQS amplification -> More ferroptosis
raw.critic_question_addressed: general: ferroptosis-to-QS direction with amplification loop

raw.title: QS-Regulated Bacterial GSH Import Creates Pericellular GSH Desert that Sensitizes Host Epithelial Cells to Ferroptosis
raw.connection: Bacterial QS activation -> Upregulated GSH import (GsiABCD) and GGT -> Pericellular GSH depletion -> Host intracellular GSH falls below GPX4 Km -> Ferroptosis sensitization
raw.critic_question_addressed: H8: alternative GSH depletion mechanism (extracellular rather than transporter-mediated)

raw.title: 4-HNE-Cysteine Thiazolidine Ring (from Bacterial GGT Processing of Exported GS-HNE) Activates SdiA as AHL Structural Mimic
raw.connection: Ferroptotic 4-HNE -> GST A4-4 conjugation -> MRP1 export of GS-HNE -> Bacterial GGT hydrolysis -> 4-HNE-Cys thiazolidine cyclization -> SdiA activation via 5-membered heterocyclic ring mimicry
raw.critic_question_addressed: H1: provides genuine 5-membered ring via biologically plausible route

raw.title: Fur-Mediated Transcriptional Rewiring Under Ferroptotic Iron Excess Decouples PQS from Iron Scavenging, Enabling Repurposing as Cytotoxic Ferroptosis-Amplification Signal
raw.connection: Ferroptotic iron/heme release -> Fur activation -> Siderophore repression + heme uptake maintenance -> PQS decoupled from iron scavenging -> PQS repurposed as ferroptosis amplifier (via CNMT-TFR1) -> Positive feedback loop
raw.critic_question_addressed: H2: directly resolves Fur repression paradox

critiqued.title: 3-oxo-C12-HSL Induces Ferroptosis via TRIM25-Mediated GPX4 Proteasomal Degradation
critiqued.key_weakness: TRIM25-GPX4 already published (N6F11, Li 2023 Sci Transl Med); NF-kB does NOT transcriptionally induce TRIM25 (TRIM25 is ISG, not NF-kB target -- causal arrow reversed); Kravchenko 2008 citation wrong journal (Science not J Biol Chem) and shows NF-kB disruption in macrophages

critiqued.title: Ferroptotic HMGB1 Release Displaces LuxR from DNA
critiqued.key_weakness: HMGB1 (25 kDa) cannot enter gram-negative bacteria (porin cutoff ~600 Da); LuxR binds major groove but HMGB1 binds minor groove; concentrations off by >100-fold; three independently fatal barriers

critiqued.title: Pyocyanin-Initiated DHODH-Pathway Ferroptosis
critiqued.key_weakness: Compartment-specific DHODH prediction may be oversimplified (pyocyanin attacks all three anti-ferroptotic axes); borderline trivially deducible from existing knowledge; pyocyanin pleiotropic effects may mask ferroptosis contribution

critiqued.title: 15-HpETE-PE Activates PqsR via Microvesicles
critiqued.key_weakness: PqsR requires aromatic quinolone ring for Tyr258 pi-pi stacking; 15-HpETE is linear eicosanoid with no aromatic ring; all known PqsR ligands are aromatic; microvesicle-bacterial membrane fusion thermodynamically unfavorable

critiqued.title: Bacterial GSH Scavenging Creates Ferroptosis-Sensitizing GSH Desert
critiqued.key_weakness: GsiABCD characterized in E. coli not P. aeruginosa; QS regulation of GSH import unverified; host GGT competes for extracellular GSH; GPX4 Km for GSH uncertain (could be 100x lower than claimed); mucus barrier reduces bacterial access

critiqued.title: 4-HNE-Cys Thiazolidine Activates SdiA as AHL Mimic
critiqued.key_weakness: H-bonding mismatch (thiazolidine NH donor vs lactone O acceptor); thiazolidine ring equilibrium reversible at physiological pH; non-AHL SdiA modulators tend to be inhibitors not agonists; SdiA pocket may not accommodate 9-carbon chain

critiqued.title: Fur-PQS Functional Switch Under Ferroptotic Iron Excess
critiqued.key_weakness: PrrF-antR pathway reduces PQS under iron excess (contradicts 'PQS unaffected' claim); PQS-Fe3+ chelation may be inseparable from cytotoxicity (undermines 'functional switch' framing); host iron re-sequestration limits temporal window

critic_questions_addressed: H8 alternative mechanism -> C2-1 (TRIM25 GPX4 degradation), C2-5 (bacterial GSH scavenging)
H2 Fur paradox -> C2-7 (PQS functional switch)
General ferroptosis-to-QS pivot -> C2-2 (HMGB1), C2-4 (15-HpETE-PE microvesicles), C2-6 (HNE-Cys thiazolidine)

critic_questions.question: Has any group published pyocyanin + ferroptosis assays (ferrostatin rescue, C11-BODIPY) between May 2025 and March 2026? This is the single highest-priority literature search for the Quality Gate.

What is the NET effect of iron excess on PQS production in P. aeruginosa, given the opposing forces of kynurenine pathway anthranilate supply (increased) vs antR-mediated anthranilate degradation (increased)? The answer determines whether the PQS amplification loop is feasible.

metadata.critique_key_discovery: 2025 Nature Communications paper shows PQS induces macrophage ferroptosis via CNMT-TFR1. QS-to-ferroptosis direction is no longer disjoint. Ferroptosis-to-QS direction remains unexplored.
critique_cycle2_key_discoveries: TRIM25-GPX4 ubiquitination already published (N6F11, Li et al. 2023 Sci Transl Med) -- kills C2-1 novelty
3-oxo-C12-HSL NF-kB effects are cell-type dependent (inhibits in macrophages, activates in epithelial cells)
PrrF-antR pathway complicates C2-7 iron-excess model -- PQS may decrease under iron excess
No pyocyanin-ferroptosis papers found (novelty of C2-3 confirmed as of March 2026 searches)
